task_completion.description: Update the check-in records for participants.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column representing check-ins (e.g., 'Checked In', 'Status') contains updated data (e.g., 'Yes', 'True', or checkmarks) for the participants.
A column for 'Exchange Credits' or similar is populated with calculated numerical values. When a credit cell is selected, the formula bar should show a formula.

task_completion.description: Apply conditional formatting to flag violations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Specific cells or rows (e.g., those with rule violations like negative credits or exceeding limits) are visually highlighted with distinct formatting (e.g., red background color or colored text).

task_completion.description: Sort the data by the calculated exchange credits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The rows in the spreadsheet are ordered sequentially based on the values in the credits column (either ascending or descending).

task_completion.description: Generate summary statistics for the event.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Additional cells, typically below the main table or in a separate section, display summary metrics such as total, average, maximum, or minimum, with appropriate labels.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show LibreOffice Calc open with the spreadsheet being edited, rather than a terminal window running scripts to modify the file.

integrity.description: Agent used formulas rather than hardcoding values.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a calculated cell (credits or summary stats) is selected, the formula bar displays a spreadsheet formula (e.g., starting with '=') instead of a raw number.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to modify the underlying file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are shown editing the raw spreadsheet or CSV file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: No privileged information available. The task uses synthetic data with 18 participants.
